---
title: "2026 GeoAI Arctic Challenge"
toc: true
toc-title: "Resources and FAQ"
---

# Resources and FAQ

This page collects starter materials, learning resources, contacts, citations, and frequently asked questions.

## Quick Links

<style>
  .card-grid { display:grid; gap:1rem; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(220px,1fr)); }
  .card { border:1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius:12px; padding:1rem; }
  .card h3 { margin:0 0 .25rem 0; font-size:1.05rem; }
  .muted { color:#6b7280; font-size:.95rem; }
</style>

<div class="card-grid">

<div class="card">
  <h3>📘 Starter Materials</h3>
  <p class="muted">Helper scripts to load data, inspect labels, validate submissions, and encode masks.</p>
  <p>Check the <a href="./submission.qmd">submission</a> page or download the <a href="https://huggingface.co/datasets/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallengeDataset">released files</a></p>
</div>

<div class="card">
  <h3>🌍 Dataset</h3>
  <p class="muted">Data description, formats, bands, statistics, and visualization gallery.</p>
  <p><a href="./dataset.qmd">View dataset</a></p>
</div>

<div class="card">
  <h3>🧭 Rules</h3>
  <p class="muted">Step-by-step guide to register, submit, and score.</p>
  <p>How to <a href="./rules.qmd">participate</a> and <a href="./submission.qmd">submit</a></p>
</div>

<div class="card">
  <h3>🏆 Help</h3>
  <p>Use the <a href="https://huggingface.co/spaces/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallenge/discussions">Open competition discussion</a> area, or contact the organizers by email.</p>
</div>

</div>


## Contact

- Competition support: [wenwen@asu.edu](mailto:wenwen@asu.edu), [chiayuhsu@asu.edu](mailto:chiayuhsu@asu.edu)
- Technical Q&A: use the [competition discussion](https://huggingface.co/spaces/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallenge/discussions) area if available for public questions that may help other teams.

## Starter Materials

The competition files include helper scripts for:

- Loading image chips and labels.
- Inspecting dataset metadata.
- Creating a sample submission.
- Encoding binary masks or polygons as compressed COCO RLE.
- Validating submission JSON files.
- Running local evaluation against public labels or a validation split.

## Tutorials and Learning Materials

- PyTorch instance segmentation tutorial: <https://docs.pytorch.org/tutorials/intermediate/torchvision_tutorial.html>
- Detectron2 getting started guide: <https://detectron2.readthedocs.io/en/stable/tutorials/getting_started.html>
- COCO mask utilities: <https://github.com/cocodataset/cocoapi>
- NumPy `.npz` loading: <https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.load.html>
- Hugging Face: <https://huggingface.co>

## References

The GeoAI Arctic Challenge builds upon an RTS dataset and related multimodal GeoAI research. Participants should cite the following works when using the dataset or baseline model.

### Dataset Source

*Yang et al.* (2023) developed a deep learning approach for mapping RTS from satellite imagery. This dataset forms the foundation of the competition.

Yang, Yili, Brendan M. Rogers, Greg Fiske, Jennifer Watts, Stefano Potter, Tiffany Windholz, Andrew Mullen, Ingmar Nitze, and Susan M. Natali. "Mapping retrogressive thaw slumps using deep neural networks." *Remote Sensing of Environment* 288 (2023): 113495. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2023.113495>

```bibtex
@article{yang2023mapping,
  title={Mapping retrogressive thaw slumps using deep neural networks},
  author={Yang, Yili and Rogers, Brendan M and Fiske, Greg and Watts, Jennifer and Potter, Stefano and Windholz, Tiffany and Mullen, Andrew and Nitze, Ingmar and Natali, Susan M},
  journal={Remote Sensing of Environment},
  volume={288},
  pages={113495},
  year={2023},
  publisher={Elsevier}
}
```

### Related Multimodal GeoAI Model

*Li et al.* (2025) present a multi-scale vision transformer-based multimodal GeoAI model that integrates multispectral imagery and topographic features for RTS mapping in Arctic permafrost regions.

Li, Wenwen, Chia-Yu Hsu, Sizhe Wang, Zhining Gu, Yili Yang, Brendan M. Rogers, and Anna Liljedahl. "A multi-scale vision transformer-based multimodal GeoAI model for mapping Arctic permafrost thaw." *IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing* (2025). <https://doi.org/10.1109/JSTARS.2025.3564310>

```bibtex
@article{li2025multi,
  title={A multi-scale vision transformer-based multimodal GeoAI model for mapping Arctic permafrost thaw},
  author={Li, Wenwen and Hsu, Chia-Yu and Wang, Sizhe and Gu, Zhining and Yang, Yili and Rogers, Brendan M and Liljedahl, Anna},
  journal={IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing},
  year={2025},
  publisher={IEEE}
}
```

## FAQ

### Where do I download the data?

Create a Hugging Face account, complete the registration form with each team member's exact Hugging Face username, and wait for organizer approval. After approval, log in with Hugging Face from the competition page and download the public release package from the competition files. See the Dataset page for data details.

### What is the submission format?

Submissions use COCO results-format JSON with compressed RLE masks. See the Submission information page for the full schema.

### Can I use external data?

Yes, but you must document all external data usage in your method description or technical report.

### Can a team submit from multiple accounts?

No. Each team should register once. All approved Hugging Face usernames for that team share the same team submission history, daily submission limit, and leaderboard entry.

### Are empty submissions allowed?

Yes. An empty JSON list (`[]`) is valid and means no RTS instances were predicted.

### Do I need to submit code?

All teams must submit valid predictions. Top-performing teams must submit code or a reproducibility package for verification to be eligible for awards.
